{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Sales Representative",
  "description": "Amani Advisory Limited is committed to assisting organizations in achieving success by maximizing their most valuable resource - their employees. We offer complete HR solutions to businesses of all sizes and in various industries. Thanks to a team of seasoned HR professionals, we approach every project with expertise, innovation, and a client-centric mindset.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Representative&nbsp;
Job Location:&nbsp;Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Work hours: 10am &ndash; 7pm

Job Summary


	The ideal candidate will be responsible for delivering an excellent in-store customer experience while achieving individual and store sales targets. The role involves product presentation, customer engagement, transaction processing, and maintaining a clean and well-organized retail environment.


Responsibilities
Sales &amp; Customer Engagement:


	Greet and respond to customer inquiries and complaints via phone, walk-in and chats (all social media platforms) with professionalism and courtesy.
	Recommend and upsell products in line with customer preferences and store promotions.
	Meet or exceed assigned daily, weekly, and monthly sales targets.
	Handle customer objections and complaints politely, escalating when necessary.


Store Operations:


	Maintain visual merchandising standards in line with brand guidelines.
	Ensure products are well-stocked, properly tagged, and attractively displayed.
	Assist with stock counts, replenishment, and inventory control.
	Process sales transactions accurately using POS systems and handle cash/card payments.


Customer Experience &amp; Retention:


	Build strong customer relationships to encourage repeat business.
	Collect customer feedback and communicate insights to management.
	Support loyalty programs, promotions, and in-store activations.


Compliance &amp; Teamwork


	Adhere to company policies, sales procedures, and loss prevention guidelines.
	Work collaboratively with team members to achieve overall store goals.
	Maintain punctuality, grooming standards, and professional conduct at all times.


Requirements


	BSc/HND in Business, Marketing, or a related field
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to learn quickly and adapt to a fast-paced sales environment.
	Confidence in speaking with potential customers and handling objections.
	Goal-oriented with a strong drive to succeed.
	Basic knowledge of Microsoft Office and CRM software is a plus.
	Prior sales or customer service experience is a plus but not mandatory.


Salary
N150,000 per month.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CVs to:&nbsp;talent@amaniadvisory.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",
  "datePosted": "2026-03-19",
  "validThrough": "2026-05-01",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Amani Advisory Limited"
    
  },
  "jobLocation": {
    "@type": "Place",
    "address": {
      "@type": "PostalAddress",
       "postalCode": ". ",
       "addressRegion": ". ",
       "streetAddress": ". ",
      "addressLocality": "Lagos",
      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },
  "identifier": {
    "@type": "PropertyValue",
    "name": "",
    "value": "196157"
  },
  "baseSalary": {
    "@type": "MonetaryAmount",
    "currency": "NGN",
    "value": {
      "@type": "QuantitativeValue",
      "minValue": 150000,
      "maxValue": 250000,
      "unitText": "MONTH"
    }
  }
}



Sales Representative Job at Amani Advisory Limited




        
    

        
        Amani Advisory Limited is committed to assisting organizations in achieving success by maximizing their most valuable resource - their employees. We offer complete HR solutions to businesses of all sizes and in various industries. Thanks to a team of seasoned HR professionals, we approach every project with expertise, innovation, and a client-centric mindset.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Representative 
Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Work hours: 10am – 7pm

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The ideal candidate will be responsible for delivering an excellent in-store customer experience while achieving individual and store sales targets. The role involves product presentation, customer engagement, transaction processing, and maintaining a clean and well-organized retail environment.


Responsibilities
Sales & Customer Engagement:


	Greet and respond to customer inquiries and complaints via phone, walk-in and chats (all social media platforms) with professionalism and courtesy.
	Recommend and upsell products in line with customer preferences and store promotions.
	Meet or exceed assigned daily, weekly, and monthly sales targets.
	Handle customer objections and complaints politely, escalating when necessary.


Store Operations:


	Maintain visual merchandising standards in line with brand guidelines.
	Ensure products are well-stocked, properly tagged, and attractively displayed.
	Assist with stock counts, replenishment, and inventory control.
	Process sales transactions accurately using POS systems and handle cash/card payments.


Customer Experience & Retention:


	Build strong customer relationships to encourage repeat business.
	Collect customer feedback and communicate insights to management.
	Support loyalty programs, promotions, and in-store activations.


Compliance & Teamwork


	Adhere to company policies, sales procedures, and loss prevention guidelines.
	Work collaboratively with team members to achieve overall store goals.
	Maintain punctuality, grooming standards, and professional conduct at all times.


Requirements


	BSc/HND in Business, Marketing, or a related field
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to learn quickly and adapt to a fast-paced sales environment.
	Confidence in speaking with potential customers and handling objections.
	Goal-oriented with a strong drive to succeed.
	Basic knowledge of Microsoft Office and CRM software is a plus.
	Prior sales or customer service experience is a plus but not mandatory.


Salary
N150,000 per month.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CVs to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Inventory Manager",
  "description": "Ibifoundry Limited is a business services and digital strategy firm.&nbsp;The company specializes in several key professional areas:&nbsp;Business Systems: Designing and implementing structured operational frameworks for organizations,&nbsp;Digital Strategy: Developing online growth and marketing plans to enhance brand presence,&nbsp;Enterprise-Level Growth: Providing expert guidance on scaling businesses and improving overall enterprise performance.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Inventory Manager
Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Devise ways to optimize inventory control procedures
	Inspect the levels of business supplies and raw material to identify shortages
	Ensure product stock is adequate for all distribution channels and can cover direct demand from customers
	Record daily deliveries and shipments to reconcile inventory
	Use software to monitor demand and document characteristics of inventory
	Place orders to replenish stock avoiding insufficiencies or excessive surplus
	Analyze data to anticipate future needs
	Evaluate suppliers to achieve cost-effective deals and maintain trust relationships
	Collaborate with warehouse employees and other staff to ensure business goals are met
	Report to upper management on stock levels, issues etc.


Requirements and skills


	BSc/BA in business administration. logistics or relevant field;
	Proven experience as inventory manager or similar position
	Excellent knowledge of data analysis and forecasting methods
	Working knowledge of inventory management software (e.g. ERP)
	Ability to accurately track inventory and create reports
	An analytical mind with strong math skills
	Excellent organizational and planning skills
	Outstanding communication and interpersonal abilities
	Reliable and trustworthy.


Salary
N120,000 - N200,000 per month.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV &nbsp;to:&nbsp;ibifoundrylimited@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Posiiton as the subject of the email.",

Inventory Manager Job at Ibifoundry Limited




        
    

        
        Ibifoundry Limited is a business services and digital strategy firm. The company specializes in several key professional areas: Business Systems: Designing and implementing structured operational frameworks for organizations, Digital Strategy: Developing online growth and marketing plans to enhance brand presence, Enterprise-Level Growth: Providing expert guidance on scaling businesses and improving overall enterprise performance.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Inventory Manager
Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Devise ways to optimize inventory control procedures
	Inspect the levels of business supplies and raw material to identify shortages
	Ensure product stock is adequate for all distribution channels and can cover direct demand from customers
	Record daily deliveries and shipments to reconcile inventory
	Use software to monitor demand and document characteristics of inventory
	Place orders to replenish stock avoiding insufficiencies or excessive surplus
	Analyze data to anticipate future needs
	Evaluate suppliers to achieve cost-effective deals and maintain trust relationships
	Collaborate with warehouse employees and other staff to ensure business goals are met
	Report to upper management on stock levels, issues etc.


Requirements and skills


	BSc/BA in business administration. logistics or relevant field;
	Proven experience as inventory manager or similar position
	Excellent knowledge of data analysis and forecasting methods
	Working knowledge of inventory management software (e.g. ERP)
	Ability to accurately track inventory and create reports
	An analytical mind with strong math skills
	Excellent organizational and planning skills
	Outstanding communication and interpersonal abilities
	Reliable and trustworthy.


Salary
N120,000 - N200,000 per month.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV  to: [email protected] using the Job Posiiton as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

  "description": "Talent Towers was founded with a clear purpose to transform the human resource landscape in Africa, Talent Towers is a people-first Human Capital Solutions firm committed to delivering innovative, practical, and impactful solutions that drive business performance and workforce excellence.

Job Location:&nbsp;Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Major Responsibilities and Skill Requirements


	Lead and manage the Dental Clinic&rsquo;s social media presence across platforms including Instagram, LinkedIn and other relevant channels.
	Develop and implement structured social media and digital marketing strategies to grow brand visibility and patient engagement.
	Plan and oversee the creation of high-quality content including educational dental content, promotional campaigns and patient awareness initiatives.
	Monitor and respond to social media enquiries and direct messages in a timely and professional manner.
	Analyse digital performance metrics including impressions, engagement, leads and patient conversions.
	Work closely with clinical staff to develop informative and authoritative dental educational content.
	Support internal marketing initiatives including membership plan promotion and patient awareness campaigns.
	Support external marketing initiatives targeting schools, corporate organisations, and community partnerships.
	Produce structured weekly and monthly performance reports analysing social media activity and marketing outcomes.

	Strong social media management and digital marketing capability
	Content planning and campaign management skills
	Ability to analyse marketing data and performance metrics
	Strong organisational and communication skills
	Ability to work independently while collaborating with clinical and administrative teams
	Professional presentation and brand awareness


Working Hours and Days:


	This is a full-time position. Working hours are Monday to Friday from 9:00am to 6:00pm.
	Some flexibility may occasionally be required depending on marketing campaigns or events.


Salary
N450,000 - N500,000 per month.&nbsp;

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;recruitment@talenttowers.ng&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Social Media & Digital Growth Business Manager Job at Talent Towers




        
    

        
        Talent Towers was founded with a clear purpose to transform the human resource landscape in Africa, Talent Towers is a people-first Human Capital Solutions firm committed to delivering innovative, practical, and impactful solutions that drive business performance and workforce excellence.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Social Media & Digital Growth Business Manager
Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Major Responsibilities and Skill Requirements
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Lead and manage the Dental Clinic’s social media presence across platforms including Instagram, LinkedIn and other relevant channels.
	Develop and implement structured social media and digital marketing strategies to grow brand visibility and patient engagement.
	Plan and oversee the creation of high-quality content including educational dental content, promotional campaigns and patient awareness initiatives.
	Monitor and respond to social media enquiries and direct messages in a timely and professional manner.
	Analyse digital performance metrics including impressions, engagement, leads and patient conversions.
	Work closely with clinical staff to develop informative and authoritative dental educational content.
	Support internal marketing initiatives including membership plan promotion and patient awareness campaigns.
	Support external marketing initiatives targeting schools, corporate organisations, and community partnerships.
	Produce structured weekly and monthly performance reports analysing social media activity and marketing outcomes.


Key Skills:


	Strong social media management and digital marketing capability
	Content planning and campaign management skills
	Ability to analyse marketing data and performance metrics
	Strong organisational and communication skills
	Ability to work independently while collaborating with clinical and administrative teams
	Professional presentation and brand awareness


Working Hours and Days:


	This is a full-time position. Working hours are Monday to Friday from 9:00am to 6:00pm.
	Some flexibility may occasionally be required depending on marketing campaigns or events.


Salary
N450,000 - N500,000 per month. 

Method of Application

title: Customer Care Representative Job at the Vault Recruitment
content:
The Vault Recruitment - Our client, a reputable company, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Care Representative

Vacancy Code: SSC002
Job Location: Marina CMS, Lagos Island - Lagos
Work Mode: On-site

Role Overview


	Our client, a reputable company located in Marina CMS, Lagos Island, is seeking a customer-focused Customer Care Representative to join their team.
	The ideal candidate must have strong communication skills and a passion for delivering excellent customer service.


Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Handle customer inquiries, complaints, and requests professionally
	Provide accurate information about products and services
	Maintain positive customer relationships and ensure customer satisfaction
	Document customer interactions and escalate issues where necessary
	Support basic administrative and customer service operations.


Requirements


	OND, HND, or BSc qualification
	Minimum of 1 year of experience in customer care, sales, or an administrative role
	Not more than 35 years old
	Must reside around Marina CMS, Lagos Island (e.g., Obalende, CMS, Ebute Metta, etc.)
	Strong verbal and written communication skills
	Customer-oriented with a professional attitude.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Management Accountant",
  "description": "Dangote Group is one of Nigeria&rsquo;s most diversified business conglomerates with a hard &ndash; earned reputation for excellent business practices and products&rsquo; quality with its operational headquarters in the bustling metropolis of Lagos, Nigeria in West Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Management Accountant&nbsp;

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos

Job Summary


	Manage and coordinate budgeting and management reporting procedures at the plant and ensure availability of up-to-date and accurate information on budgeted and actual financial activities, to support informed decision-making by management.


Key Duties and Responsibilities


	Circulate the annual business planning guidelines/ assumptions and provide guidance to budget holders during the planning process the plant.
	Oversee collation of budget inputs across the plant and prepare consolidated annual budget for the plant.
	Monitor approved budget allocations by obtaining information on revenue and expenditure, and reporting variances on a monthly basis.
	Drive periodic budget forecasting and recommend budget review as required.
	Develop periodic and other ad hoc budget reports, forecasts and analysis.
	Generate monthly business performance statements, noting major deviations from planned budget and risk areas and prepare variance analysis for Head, Plant Finance.
	Manage journal entries on SAP (or other system) and check with relevant documents (e.g. GRN/ SRN, invoices, etc.) to ensure uniformity of details and accuracy of postings/ reconciliations for month-end or periodic management reporting.
	Approve vouchers and journals for posting and manage reconciliation to various ledgers.
	Allocate costs for central or joint expenditure to relevant cost centres in line with DCP&rsquo;s cost allocation policies.
	Proffer relevant recommendations to the Head, Plant Finance on the financial implications and consequences of business decisions at the plant.
	Provide Plant Management with complete, valid, accurate and timely reports for effective decision making.
	Perform any other duties as may be assigned by the Head, Plant Finance.


Requirements
Education and Work Experience:


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree or its equivalent in Accounting, Finance, Economics or other related discipline.
	Relevant professional certification (e.g. ACA, ACCA, CIMA, CFA).
	Minimum of ten (10) years progressive HR work experience.


Skills and Competencies:


	Good understanding of trends, challenges, opportunities, regulations and legislations relating to the cement manufacturing industry.
	Proven experience in planning, budgeting and forecasting activities.
	Very sound business acumen &ndash; financial and commercial knowledge.
	Strong communication and presentation skills.
	Good leadership and people management skills.
	Strong problem solving skills.
	High ethical standards and integrity.
	Ability to manage multiple priorities.
	Working knowledge/ experience in the use of financial systems including SAP.
	Good understanding of micro and macroeconomic indices and their impact on the cement business.
	Intermediate to advanced knowledge of IFRS, finance best practices and other applicable reporting and accounting standards.


Benefits


	Private Health Insurance
	Paid Time Off
	Training &amp; Development.

Management Accountant Job at Dangote Group




        
    

        
        Dangote Group is one of Nigeria’s most diversified business conglomerates with a hard – earned reputation for excellent business practices and products’ quality with its operational headquarters in the bustling metropolis of Lagos, Nigeria in West Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Management Accountant 

Job Location: Lagos

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Manage and coordinate budgeting and management reporting procedures at the plant and ensure availability of up-to-date and accurate information on budgeted and actual financial activities, to support informed decision-making by management.


Key Duties and Responsibilities


	Circulate the annual business planning guidelines/ assumptions and provide guidance to budget holders during the planning process the plant.
	Oversee collation of budget inputs across the plant and prepare consolidated annual budget for the plant.
	Monitor approved budget allocations by obtaining information on revenue and expenditure, and reporting variances on a monthly basis.
	Drive periodic budget forecasting and recommend budget review as required.
	Develop periodic and other ad hoc budget reports, forecasts and analysis.
	Generate monthly business performance statements, noting major deviations from planned budget and risk areas and prepare variance analysis for Head, Plant Finance.
	Manage journal entries on SAP (or other system) and check with relevant documents (e.g. GRN/ SRN, invoices, etc.) to ensure uniformity of details and accuracy of postings/ reconciliations for month-end or periodic management reporting.
	Approve vouchers and journals for posting and manage reconciliation to various ledgers.
	Allocate costs for central or joint expenditure to relevant cost centres in line with DCP’s cost allocation policies.
	Proffer relevant recommendations to the Head, Plant Finance on the financial implications and consequences of business decisions at the plant.
	Provide Plant Management with complete, valid, accurate and timely reports for effective decision making.
	Perform any other duties as may be assigned by the Head, Plant Finance.


Requirements
Education and Work Experience:


	Bachelor’s Degree or its equivalent in Accounting, Finance, Economics or other related discipline.
	Relevant professional certification (e.g. ACA, ACCA, CIMA, CFA).
	Minimum of ten (10) years progressive HR work experience.


Skills and Competencies:


	Good understanding of trends, challenges, opportunities, regulations and legislations relating to the cement manufacturing industry.
	Proven experience in planning, budgeting and forecasting activities.
	Very sound business acumen – financial and commercial knowledge.
	Strong communication and presentation skills.
	Good leadership and people management skills.
	Strong problem solving skills.
	High ethical standards and integrity.
	Ability to manage multiple priorities.
	Working knowledge/ experience in the use of financial systems including SAP.
	Good understanding of micro and macroeconomic indices and their impact on the cement business.
	Intermediate to advanced knowledge of IFRS, finance best practices and other applicable reporting and accounting standards.


Benefits


	Private Health Insurance
	Paid Time Off
	Training & Development.
    

    

        Click here to Apply
